sa:items.rdfs:label: Glyphthread Undergarments
sa:items.sa:description: Breathable underlayers woven with hygienic glyphs. They prevent odor, repair themselves, and adjust to temperature changes.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Worn by citizens across all climates, professions, and stations.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Has virtually eliminated sweat and laundry from everyday life, shifting cleanliness standards and class signals.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Whispering Contacts
sa:items.sa:description: Disposable magical lenses that allow subvocal communication within 1 km of another paired user.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Used by professionals, students, lovers, and criminals.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Redefined how people argue, cheat, or conspire — now done with barely a twitch.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Soulmark Authenticator
sa:items.sa:description: A personal magic ID embedded in jewelry or subdermal crystals, tied to your soul's magical signature.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Used for personal security, unlocking devices, and accessing bank accounts.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Has replaced keys and passwords. Also allows state or corporate tracking — a concern in surveillance-heavy societies.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Arcanodex
sa:items.sa:description: A portable device housing knowledge-spirits, capable of answering questions, casting simple diagnostics, or streaming spells.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Carried by students, mages, mechanics, and bureaucrats.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Encourages magical literacy but fosters dependence. Known to cause 'arcanodex rot' — a decline in deep magical understanding.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Nullbrick Paint
sa:items.sa:description: A magical paint additive that blocks teleportation, scrying, and phase intrusion through walls.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Applied in homes, boardrooms, vaults, and panic rooms.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Standard in modern construction. Also weaponized by criminals to hide from leyline surveillance.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Mourning Candle
sa:items.sa:description: A grief-absorbing candle that eases emotional trauma and preserves emotional echoes of the departed.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Used in funerals, hospice care, and front-line barracks.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Helps emotional healing but has led to reliance and emotional numbing in some cultures.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Snapshade Halo
sa:items.sa:description: A stylish circlet that subtly alters facial expressions, tone, and appearance using illusion magic.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Popular with influencers, job-seekers, and the socially anxious.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Blurs the line between truth and presentation, affecting trust in social interactions.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Leywave Charger
sa:items.sa:description: A flat platform that charges magical or hybrid devices using ambient ley energy.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Standard fixture in homes, vehicles, and cafes.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Provides near-universal power access — and an opportunity for leyline siphoning and power theft.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Synthesis Beacon (Skyhaven)
sa:items.sa:description: An ornate crystalline familiar issued by the Luminous Synthesis. It tracks your arcane lineage, spell affinity signatures, and Manfuctor apprenticeship status. Grants access to restricted academies and magical districts. Broadcasts subtle aura indicating your magical achievement tier to other Synthesis members.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Given to all Skyhaven residents upon registration or academic enrollment.
sa:items.societalImpact: Creates a hierarchy of magical privilege. Refusal or loss of the beacon marks one as spiritually incomplete or magically unworthy—a social catastrophe. Synthesis uses broadcasts to predict talent and poach competitors.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Syndicate Collar (Ironhold)
sa:items.sa:description: A reinforced micro-familiar worn as an armband or neck piece. Tracks shift hours, location within work zones, health vitals, and safety compliance. Alerts workers to hazardous conditions and coordinates rescue in industrial accidents. Grants bonus to saves against exhaustion and disease.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Mandatory for all Forge Syndicate workers. Issued upon employment; removed only upon termination or death.
sa:items.societalImpact: Presents as worker protection but enables exploitation tracking. Union uses data to audit productivity and identify 'slackers.' Workers dependent on it for incident response—removing it is career suicide. Creates surveillance-based safety culture.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Mask Whisper (Blackwater)
sa:items.sa:description: An elusive construct that phases between visibility and shadow, appearing as a small iridescent token or flickering mote. It relays encrypted messages between members of the Velvet Mask and alerts wearers to underground movement, safe houses, and shadow-market activity. Grants advantage on checks to navigate black-market networks.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Distributed by the Velvet Mask to syndicate members, informants, and approved operators.
sa:items.societalImpact: Symbol of inclusion in shadow networks; exclusion from it is a death sentence. Enables organized crime coordination and information brokering. Possession signals to enforcement that you're 'connected'—protection and entrapment.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Research Echo (Emerald Heights)
sa:items.sa:description: A living botanical construct resembling a luminescent vine or floating seed pod. It records ambient ecological data, organism encounters, and research observations. Syncs with the Verdant Collective's databases to grant advantage on Investigation checks related to xenobiology and grants access to collective research archives.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Issued to all researchers, graduate students, and naturalists enrolled with the Verdant Collective.
sa:items.societalImpact: Purports to democratize research but actually centralizes knowledge control. All discoveries feed the Collective's monopoly on biotech patents. Contributes to anti-competitive pressure on independent scientists.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Commerce Cipher (Starfall)
sa:items.sa:description: A portable crystalline node that broadcasts trade credentials, tariff clearance, diplomatic immunity (if applicable), and shipping status. Grants advantage on Commerce and negotiation checks. Automatically translates Concordat protocols and reveals current trade law updates.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Issued to merchants, traders, and diplomatic representatives operating in Starfall's markets and orbital facilities.
sa:items.societalImpact: Without it, you're a non-resident facing tariff penalties and legal liability. Effectively creates a two-tier economy: authorized traders and black-market operators. Concordat uses cipher data to track commodity flows and manipulate prices.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Altitude Warden (Arcech-Sylandris)
sa:items.sa:description: An elegant, status-sensitive construct that shifts appearance based on vertical authorization level. Lower-tier versions glow faintly; upper-tier versions radiate crystalline light. Grants or restricts access to maglev lines, elevator spires, and altitude zones. Broadcasts your current altitude-class to other Warden wearers. Syncs with House Valorian's real estate management network.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Distributed by House Valorian to all Arcech-Sylandris residents. Appearance and function tied to property ownership and financial standing.
sa:items.societalImpact: Institutionalizes verticality as social identity. Lower-altitude residents are visibly marked as subordinate; denial of upgrade access is a form of economic punishment. Creates aspirational consumption—people pursue altitude upgrades as social destiny. Loss of the Warden can trigger eviction or exile to 'the Roots.'

sa:items.rdfs:label: Sundraft Sachet
sa:items.sa:description: A single-use climate pouch that warms or cools a small room for an hour.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Thrown at dinner parties, work meetings, and commuter pods.
sa:items.sa:societalImpact: Climate control is ritualized and cultural. Known to be used romantically and politically.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Whispershade Mantle
sa:items.sa:description: A sleek, shimmering cloak that muffles your movements and cloaks your outline in soft shadow. Grants advantage on Stealth checks in dim light or darker.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Favored by spies, thieves, and diplomatic bodyguards.
sa:items.societalImpact: Often used by corporate agents and rogue influencers. Wearing one is both a fashion statement and a warning.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Mnemonic Prism
sa:items.sa:description: A small crystal cube that passively records the last minute of your conscious sensory experience. Once per sleep cycle, you can activate it to replay a 60-second scene with perfect clarity. Grants advantage on Insight or Investigation rolls when referencing the memory.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Used by detectives, students, and courtroom witnesses.
sa:items.societalImpact: Trust in human memory is fading. Some cultures require the use of mnemonic recordings during testimony.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Consumer Cipher
sa:items.sa:description: A subtle smart-jewelry interface that broadcasts your consumption profile, payment history, and preference signature to networked merchants and service vendors. Grants advantage on Persuasion checks during transactions and access to 'personalized' deals. Merchants instantly adjust pricing and offers to maximize extraction from your specific budget and desires.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Worn by frequent travelers, wealthy consumers, and anyone integrated into the corporate economy. Corporate loyalty programs often issue them as status markers.
sa:items.societalImpact: Promised convenience and customized service, but creates algorithmic profiling that inflates prices for high-value targets. Brands weaponize the data to trigger consumption and exploit weaknesses. Refusal to wear one marks you as 'unpredictable' or 'cash-restricted'—a social liability.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Pulseweave Gloves
sa:items.sa:description: Fingerless gloves that sync with your biofield. Once per rest cycle, you can channel energy through them to stabilize a dying creature or deal +1d6 force damage on a successful melee hit.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Popular among field medics and melee spellcasters.
sa:items.societalImpact: Their dual-purpose nature makes them popular among both healers and brawlers. Some cultures see their use as ethically complex.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Tuning Spike
sa:items.sa:description: A collapsible tuning fork carved from dragonbone and aethersteel. When planted in the ground and activated, it emits a low hum that disrupts chaotic magical effects. Grants advantage on saving throws against wild magic or environmental spell effects within 10 ft (once per sleep cycle).
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Used by expedition teams and planar surveyors.
sa:items.societalImpact: Standard issue for those traveling through unstable zones. Sometimes seen as a warding charm or good-luck ritual.

sa:items.rdfs:label: Gilded Mirrorbadge
sa:items.sa:description: A decorative insignia that reflects both light and intent. Once per day, when targeted by a charm, you may reflect the effect back to the caster as a reaction. Also grants a +1 bonus to saves against Enchantment effects.
sa:items.sa:commonUsage: Worn by noble courtiers, dueling diplomats, and counter-espionage agents.
sa:items.societalImpact: Symbol of status and paranoia in high circles. Knockoffs abound but don’t function properly.
